name: Kolaches (Kolacky)
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine the warm water, yeast, sugar, and salt.
2. Melt the butter in a pan. When melted, add 1 cup of cold water to the yeast mixture.
3. Add the eggs; mix well.
4. Gradually add 2 cups of flour, mixing thoroughly after each addition.
5. Continue adding more flour (up to 4 additional cups), beating well after each addition.
6. Cover the bowl tightly and place it in the refrigerator for at least 4-5 hours or overnight.
7. Preheat the oven to 425°F.
8. Grease cookie sheets with butter.
9. Roll dough into walnut-sized balls and place them on the prepared cookie sheets, about an inch apart.
10. Cover the tops of the balls and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (about 20 minutes).
11. Make a small indentation in the center of each ball and fill with about one teaspoon of filling.
12. Let the filled kolaches rise until spongy, about 20 minutes.
13. Bake at 425°F for 5-6 minutes or until lightly browned.
14. Sprinkle powdered sugar over the kolaches after removing them from the oven.
Fillings (thanks to Mrs. Albert Soukup, Sr.)
Traditional fillings:
Poppyseed Filling: Bring 2 cups of milk or water to a boil. Stir in 1/2 lb. ground poppyseeds. Let simmer for 20 minutes, stirring frequently. Add 3/4 cup sugar, 1 tablespoon white corn syrup, and 1/4 teaspoon salt. Simmer a few more minutes. Remove from heat and add 1/2 teaspoon almond extract, 2 tablespoons butter, 1 teaspoon vanilla, and 2 crushed graham crackers.
Prune Filling: Cook 1 lb. dried prunes until soft. Drain; remove pits. Mash and add 1/2 cup sugar, 1/2 teaspoon cinnamon, and a little vanilla.
Apricot Filling: Soak 1 package of dried apricots overnight in water (just enough to cover). Cook until soft. If any water remains, drain well. Mash, adding sugar to taste.
